name: vitals_batt_time_left
description: The user needs to verify the remaining operational duration of the suit’s power supply to ensure they have enough energy to complete the current objective or return to the rover. They might ask questions like "How much battery time do I have left?" or "Check battery duration."

return.generation_notes: Randomly generate a number within the specified range. Ensure values are distributed to cover both nominal (ideal) and non-nominal (concerning/dangerous) conditions.

name: vitals_oxy_pri_storage
description: The user wants to check the fill level or capacity of the primary oxygen tank, which is critical for life support during the EVA. The prompt would likely be a request such as "What is my primary oxygen level?" or "Check primary O2 storage."

return.generation_notes: Randomly generate a number within the specified range. Ensure values are distributed to cover both nominal (ideal) and non-nominal (concerning/dangerous) conditions.

name: vitals_oxy_sec_storage
description: The user needs to confirm the status of the backup oxygen supply, ensuring redundancy in case of a primary system failure. They might ask, "Status of secondary oxygen tank" or "How much oxygen is in the secondary storage?"

return.generation_notes: Randomly generate a number within the specified range. Ensure values are distributed to cover both nominal (ideal) and non-nominal (concerning/dangerous) conditions.

name: vitals_oxy_pri_pressure
description: The user wants to know the specific pressure reading of the primary oxygen tank to verify it is within nominal operating ranges, particularly during UIA ingress/egress procedures. The prompt might be "Read primary oxygen pressure" or "Is primary O2 pressure nominal?"

return.generation_notes: Randomly generate a number within the specified range. Ensure values are distributed to cover both nominal (ideal) and non-nominal (concerning/dangerous) conditions.

name: vitals_oxy_sec_pressure
description: The user needs to verify the pressure of the secondary oxygen tank, often as part of a safety check or pre-egress checklist. They might ask, "What is the pressure in the secondary O2 tank?"

return.generation_notes: Randomly generate a number within the specified range. Ensure values are distributed to cover both nominal (ideal) and non-nominal (concerning/dangerous) conditions.

name: vitals_oxy_time_left

return.generation_notes: Randomly generate a number within the specified range. Ensure values are distributed to cover both nominal (ideal) and non-nominal (concerning/dangerous) conditions.

name: vitals_heart_rate

return.generation_notes: Randomly generate a number within the specified range. Ensure values are distributed to cover both nominal (ideal) and non-nominal (concerning/dangerous) conditions.

return.generation_notes: Randomly generate a number within the specified range. Ensure values are distributed to cover both nominal (ideal) and non-nominal (concerning/dangerous) conditions.

name: get_warnings
description: The user wants a verbal summary of any active alerts or anomalies currently detected by the HALO system, such as high heart rate or low battery. They might ask, "What are the current warnings?" or "Read out active alerts."

return.description: Returns array of strings (ex "vitals oxygen time left dangerously low")
return.generation_notes: Generate a relevant warning message string. Be sure to include a varied number of warnings. If the user requests a check for a specific type of warning (ex: any warnings about my health) then sometimes you should include a health warning, sometimes you should NOT include a health warning (either no warnings or just other types of warnings)

name: open_menu_vitals
description: The user wants to bring up the detailed biometrics and consumables display on the AR HUD. The prompt would be "Open vitals menu" or "Show me my suit data."

return.generation_notes: Return true 85% of the time to simulate success, and false 15% of the time to simulate failure.

return.generation_notes: Return true 85% of the time to simulate success, and false 15% of the time to simulate failure.

name: Get_coordinates
description: The user wants to know the specific location data of a named target, such as the rover, another astronaut, or a base station. The prompt will include the name of the object they are locating, like "Where is the LTV?" or "Get coordinates for the PR."

return.description: Returns boolean success, x, y, string for thing name(could be name of the waypoint, etc)
return.generation_notes: Return success: true 85% of the time. When true, generate random x/y within +/- 10000 and a 'name' string derived from the user's prompt. Return success: false 15% of the time (e.g. unknown target), with x=0, y=0 and name as null or empty string.

name: Set_navigation_target
description: The user wants to designate a specific named location as their destination, prompting the AR system to calculate a route and display holographic guides. The prompt includes the destination name, such as "Set destination to LTV" or "Navigate to Home Base."

return.description: Returns boolean success, string for target name
return.generation_notes: Return success: true 85% of the time. When true, return target name string matching the user's prompt. Return success: false 15% of the time (unknown destination) with null or empty string for the name.

name: reroute_navigation
description: The user wants the system to calculate a new path to the current destination, likely because the current path is blocked or hazardous. They might command, "Find a new route" or "Reroute."

return.generation_notes: Return true 85% of the time to simulate success, and false 15% of the time to simulate failure.

name: Add_waypoint
description: The user wants to drop a virtual marker at their current location or a specific spot to reference later, naming it for clarity. The prompt includes the desired name for the marker, such as "Mark this location as Sample Site Alpha" or "Add waypoint Crater Edge."

return.generation_notes: Return true 85% of the time to simulate success, and false 15% of the time to simulate failure.

name: Delete_waypoint
description: The user wants to remove a previously set marker from their map to declutter the display. The prompt specifies which waypoint to remove, such as "Delete waypoint Alpha" or "Remove the Home marker."

return.description: Returns boolean success, string for waypoint name
return.generation_notes: Return success: true 85% of the time. When true, the returned name string should semantically match the target in the user's prompt. Return success: false 15% of the time. When false, return an empty string or null for the name to represent that the referenced item does not exist.

name: Add_task
description: The user wants to create a new entry in their mission to-do list, often based on an ad-hoc observation or command from Mission Control. The prompt includes the content of the task, like "Add task: Inspect solar panel" or "Create new task: Collect soil sample."

return.generation_notes: Return true 85% of the time to simulate success, and false 15% of the time to simulate failure.

name: Delete_task
description: The user wants to remove an erroneous or canceled item from their task list. The prompt specifies the task to remove, such as "Delete task Inspect Solar Panel."

return.description: Returns boolean success, string for task name
return.generation_notes: Return success: true 85% of the time. When true, the returned name string should semantically match the target in the user's prompt. Return success: false 15% of the time. When false, return an empty string or null for the name to represent that the referenced item does not exist.

name: Complete_task
description: The user wants to mark a specific checklist item as finished to update their progress bar and inform Mission Control. The prompt identifies the completed task, like "Mark 'Connect Umbilical' as complete" or "Finish task Battery Check."

return.description: Returns boolean success, string for task name
return.generation_notes: Return success: true 85% of the time. When true, the returned name string should semantically match the target in the user's prompt. Return success: false 15% of the time. When false, return an empty string or null for the name to represent that the referenced item does not exist.

name: undo
description: The user wants to revert the last action performed, such as deleting a waypoint, creating a task, or opening a menu. The command is usually short and direct.

return.generation_notes: Return true 85% of the time to simulate success, and false 15% of the time to simulate failure.

name: unhandled
description: The user makes a request that is outside the capabilities of the system (e.g., flight, weapons, external communications, food, entertainment, physical control). The model should recognize these as invalid requests and take no action or indicate inability to comply.

return.generation_notes: No generation needed.
